From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Ongoing armed conflict in West Asia
This article documents a current military conflict and may change rapidly. Please update outdated or incomplete information with citations to reliable sources. (March 2026) (Learn how and when to remove this message)

2026 Iran war
Part of the Middle Eastern crisis (2023–present)
Locations struck by:[15][16][17]
  • Israel and United States
  • Iran, Hezbollah, and PMF
Date28 February 2026 – present (5 days)
Location
  • West Asia[r]
  • Indian Ocean
Status Ongoing
  • Assassinations of Iranian officials, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
  • Establishment of the Interim Leadership Council in Iran's government due to Khamenei's death
  • Closure of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran's military
  • Mobilization of European militaries to defend against Iranian strikes on Cyprus
  • Hezbollah strikes on Israel trigger renewed wave of Israeli strikes on Lebanon
Belligerents
  • Israel
  • United States

  • Azerbaijan
    • Nakhchivan[a]
  • Bahrain[b]
  • Iraq[c]
    • Kurdistan Region[d]
  • Jordan[e]

    • Turkey[g]

  • Qatar[i][7]
  • Saudi Arabia[j]
  • United Arab Emirates[k]
  • United Kingdom
    • Akrotiri and Dhekelia[l]

Defensive deployments:[m]

  • France[n]
  • Greece[o]
  • Italy[p]
  • Netherlands[p]
  • Spain[p]
  • United Kingdom[q]

    • Iran
    • Hezbollah (since 2 March)
    • Palestinian Islamic Jihad (since 2 March)
    • Popular Mobilization Forces
      • Islamic Resistance in Iraq[12]
    • Islamic Group (since 4 March)[13]

Attacked by Israel:

  • Lebanon[14][disputed – discuss]

Casualties and losses
  • Per Israel and US:
  •  Israel:
    • 12 civilians killed[18]
    • 11 missing[15]
    • 1,473 injured[19]
    • 2 military personnel injured[20]
  •  United States:
    • 6 military personnel killed[s]
    • 20 injured[t]
    • For aviation losses see this list
  •  Kurdistan:
    • 1 fighter killed, 3 injured[27]
  • Per Iran (unverified):
  •  United States:
    • 650 soldiers killed or wounded[28][disputed – discuss]
    • 1 AN/FPS-132 destroyed[29][disputed – discuss]
    • 1 THAAD radar destroyed[30][disputed – discuss]
  •  Kuwait:
    • 2 naval personnel killed[31]
    • 3 military personnel injured[32]
  • Per Israel and US:
  • >3,000 military personnel killed[u]
  • 20 warships and 1 submarine sunk[v]
  • For aviation losses see this list
  • Per Iran:
  • 1,046 people killed[37]
  • Per Iranian Red Crescent:[38]
  • 1,230 killed[w]
    Per Human Rights Activists in Iran:[43]
  • 1,114 civilians killed
  •  Popular Mobilization Forces: 20 fighters killed, dozens injured[44]
  • 102 people killed, 638 injured, 83,000+ displaced in Lebanon[45][46]
  •  Hezbollah: 12 fighters arrested[38]

Middle Eastern crisis (2023–present)

  • Alim Abdallah

  • Saleh al-Arouri
  • Ali Hussein Barji
  • Razi Mousavi

  • Mushtaq Talib Al-Saeedi
  • Mohammad Reza Zahedi
  • Fuad Shukr

  • Ibrahim Aqil
